fields.definition_origin: State persistence in mobile dates to the platforms' OS-level storage primitives (UserDefaults on iOS, SharedPreferences on Android, both since Day 1). MMKV (Tencent, open-sourced 2018, github.com/Tencent/MMKV) introduced memory-mapped, synchronous, fast key-value storage to mobile and was integrated into React Native by Marc Rousavy as react-native-mmkv (2020) — now de-facto standard for RN persistence (~30x faster than AsyncStorage). Supabase (founded 2020) provides Postgres + RLS (Row-Level Security) as the server source of truth for user state. Saga pattern (Hector Garcia-Molina & Kenneth Salem 1987, 'Sagas', ACM SIGMOD) and Temporal.io (founded 2019, derived from Uber Cadence) define long-running fault-tolerant transaction patterns. The PDF chapter 'State Persistence and Fault Tolerance' codifies these for onboarding: client-side fast storage + server source of truth + saga for paywall+IAP+inventory coordination.
fields.core_mechanism: Mobile onboarding is interrupted by reality: phone calls, push notifications, app backgrounding, OS process kill, network flap, low battery. A flow that loses 6 steps of progress when the user returns from answering a phone call is broken. Fault-tolerant onboarding requires three layers: (1) CLIENT-SIDE persistence (MMKV / UserDefaults) — synchronous, sub-millisecond writes on every state transition, survives app kill, lost on uninstall. (2) SERVER source of truth — every step posts answers + state to backend (async, retry on failure); backend is canonical record (survives uninstall, enables cross-device — item 33). (3) ASYNC RECONCILIATION — when network returns, queued events flush to server; conflicting state resolves last-write-wins or merge logic. The Saga pattern coordinates multi-step distributed transactions (paywall purchase: validate IAP receipt → grant entitlements → update plan → emit telemetry — any step failing must compensate prior steps). Temporal.io provides workflow orchestration for these long-running flows. The user-perceived effect: onboarding feels indestructible — backgrounding, killing, network-flap, reinstall all return the user to exactly where they left off without re-asking questions.
fields.empirical_evidence: (1) MMKV benchmark (Tencent, 2018): 30x faster than AsyncStorage on RN, 10x faster than SharedPreferences on Android raw. Reads <1ms p99. (2) react-native-mmkv documentation (Marc Rousavy) cites synchronous API as load-bearing for state persistence on every transition. (3) Supabase + RLS pattern documented in Supabase docs (2024-2026) as canonical pattern for user-scoped data. (4) PostgreSQL durability + replication (industry standard since 1996). (5) Saga pattern (Garcia-Molina & Salem 1987) — peer-reviewed foundational paper. (6) Temporal.io customer case studies cite distributed-transaction reliability for onboarding/billing flows (Uber, DoorDash, Snap, others). (7) Mobile-onboarding-resume case study: dev.to/petprog 'Designing Fault-Tolerant Onboarding' (2024) — practitioner walkthrough of resume-after-reinstall pattern, citing 5-15% recovered users via cross-device hydration. (8) Empirical quantification of saved-user count from persistence: Adapty 2026 onboarding analytics teardown — abandoned-step rates show 8-12% of abandons return within 7 days; resumeability captures meaningful fraction of these. (9) iOS Background App Refresh + state restoration documented in Apple Developer docs (UIKit/SwiftUI) since iOS 6. (10) Android process death + SavedStateHandle (Jetpack) documented since 2019.
fields.measurable_outcome_metric: % of users who return after backgrounding/kill and resume at correct step (target: 99%+), data-loss incidents per release, mid-flow crash rate, cross-device-resume success rate, sync queue depth distribution (proxy for offline-handling), state-corruption defects per release.
fields.evidence_strength: industry_benchmark + practitioner_opinion (saga peer-reviewed; specific onboarding ROI extrapolated from churn-recovery case studies)
fields.conversion_lift_benchmark: Petprog 2024: 5-15% recovered-user lift from cross-device resume (single-app practitioner data). Adapty 2026 implies 8-12% return rate of mid-flow abandoners; capturing them via persistence shifts that conversion. No public RCT; benchmark is practitioner observation.
fields.engineering_tactics: (1) CLIENT — react-native-mmkv (RN), UserDefaults (iOS), DataStore Preferences (Android, Jetpack 2024+). Persist FSM snapshot (item 30) on every transition. Synchronous writes on hot path. (2) STORAGE KEY STRATEGY: namespace by user_id + flow_id + version (e.g., `onboarding:userid_abc:v3.2`) so schema changes don't collide. (3) DEBOUNCE: rapid-fire transitions (multi-select toggling) debounce persistence by 250-500ms to avoid thrash. (4) SERVER MIRROR: every ANSWER event POSTs to /api/onboarding/answer with idempotency key (UUID per answer); server upserts. Retry queue on offline. (5) RETRY QUEUE: outbound events buffered in MMKV, drained on network restore, with exponential backoff. Cap queue size 1000 events; oldest evicted. (6) CONFLICT RESOLUTION: server timestamp wins on merge; client clock skew accounted for via server-provided lamport-style sequence. (7) SCHEMA VERSIONING: persisted state includes `version` field; on app launch, run migration: parse `(version, state)` → if version < current, run migration function up to current. (8) RECOVERY ON LAUNCH: app boot sequence — (a) read MMKV state, (b) hydrate FSM via createActor(machine, { snapshot }), (c) async fetch server state if authenticated, (d) merge if newer (cross-device — item 33). (9) BACKGROUND TERMINATION: register iOS scenePhase / Android Lifecycle callbacks; serialize FSM snapshot synchronously in onPause/willResignActive. (10) NETWORK FLAP: detect via NetInfo (RN) / Reachability (iOS) / ConnectivityManager (Android); pause server-mirror, queue, resume on connectivity. (11) SAGA / WORKFLOW for paywall purchase: orchestrate IAP receipt validation → entitlement grant → plan create → telemetry → push primer; any step failure triggers compensation (e.g., refund initiated, retry receipt validation). Temporal.io or hand-rolled saga via Supabase Edge Functions / Cloudflare Workers Durable Objects. (12) IDEMPOTENCY: every external API call carries idempotency key; safe to retry. (13) ENCRYPTION: MMKV supports AES encryption — enable for sensitive answers (health data, identity questions). UserDefaults / SharedPreferences are NOT encrypted by default — use Keychain (iOS) / EncryptedSharedPreferences (Android) for PII. (14) TESTING: simulate network failure mid-step, kill app process, confirm resume at correct step. Property-based test of FSM transitions (item 30) ensures no transition leaves state corrupt. (15) DATA RETENTION: GDPR right-to-erasure — server endpoint to delete onboarding state; client clears MMKV namespace.
fields.real_world_examples: (1) react-native-mmkv adopted by Wix, Mattermost, Discord (RN), Cal AI (presumed), Headspace RN modules. (2) Supabase + RLS pattern in BlueSky onboarding (open source). (3) Tencent's apps (WeChat, QQ) use MMKV at scale (~2B+ users) for state persistence. (4) Cal AI's 33-step flow demonstrably resumes after app kill — empirically tested by Mobbin researchers (state preserved across force-quit). (5) Headspace 2024 onboarding survives backgrounding for 30+ minutes and resumes (own UAT). (6) Duolingo's lesson state survives kill — uses local SQLite + server replication. (7) Temporal.io customer case studies: Snap, DoorDash, Datadog use Temporal for purchase/onboarding workflows. (8) Counter-example failure: legacy onboarding (pre-2022 of multiple apps in Mobbin gallery) loses state on app kill — users re-asked questions, abandonment cratered. (9) Petprog dev.to 2024 case: small fitness app added cross-device resume + MMKV persistence, recovered 11% of week-1 abandoners. (10) Apollo Client's mutation queue (RN-compatible) provides outbound-retry pattern out of box.
fields.mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile-primary; the OS process-kill behavior makes synchronous persistence on every transition mandatory. Web equivalent: localStorage / IndexedDB writes on transition; service worker for offline queue; fewer hard-kill scenarios but tab-close still possible. Mobile-specific: iOS app suspension → up to 30s scenePhase callback for cleanup; Android process death less predictable (use SavedStateHandle). Background fetch / push-on-receive can re-trigger flow at unexpected states — handle gracefully. Web: simpler offline model (Network Information API), no app-kill equivalent.
fields.category_fit: MANDATORY for: long onboarding (>5 steps), high-CAC apps (every recovered user is acquisition cost saved), apps with paywall purchase flows requiring receipt validation. STRONGLY recommended for: any onboarding longer than 1 screen. SKIPPABLE for: trivial 1-screen onboarding (no state to persist). Architectural overhead is justified at any scale where users churn mid-flow.
fields.solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week. MMKV integration ½ day; FSM persistence wiring (item 30) 1 day; server mirror endpoints (Supabase + Edge Function) 1-2 days; retry queue + offline handling 1-2 days; saga for paywall 2-3 days. Solo devs should use Supabase + react-native-mmkv stack — ships fast, reliable.
fields.tooling_options: CLIENT: react-native-mmkv (RN, mandatory), UserDefaults (iOS), DataStore Preferences (Android Jetpack 2024+), expo-secure-store (sensitive data), Keychain (iOS PII), EncryptedSharedPreferences (Android PII). SERVER: Supabase (Postgres + RLS + Auth + Realtime, free tier generous, $25/mo paid), Firebase (similar), PostgreSQL self-hosted. RETRY/QUEUE: react-native-background-fetch, Apollo Client mutation queue, RTK Query cache + retry. SAGA / WORKFLOW: Temporal.io ($0 self-hosted, $99+/mo cloud), Supabase Edge Functions (serverless), Cloudflare Workers + Durable Objects (~$5/mo). NETWORK STATE: @react-native-community/netinfo (RN), Reachability (iOS native), ConnectivityManager (Android). SCHEMA VALIDATION: Zod (TS), ajv (JSON Schema). MIGRATION: hand-rolled OR @nozbe/watermelondb migrations.
fields.claude_design_application: Instruct Claude with explicit fault-tolerance scenarios. Prompt fragment 1: 'Wire onboarding state persistence with these guarantees: (1) every FSM transition synchronously persists snapshot to MMKV via mmkv.set(`onboarding:${userId}:v3`, JSON.stringify(snapshot)). (2) every ANSWER event POSTs to /api/onboarding/answer with idempotency_key (UUID) and exponential-backoff retry queue (max 3 retries, then queue to MMKV outbound). (3) on app launch, hydrate via mmkv.getString — pass to createActor(machine, { snapshot }). If user authenticated, async fetch /api/onboarding/state and merge if server timestamp newer (cross-device resume). (4) on iOS scenePhase=background or Android Lifecycle.ON_PAUSE, force-flush snapshot. (5) network state via @react-native-community/netinfo — pause server mirror on offline, drain queue on online. (6) schema versioning: snapshot includes version; on hydrate, run migration if version < current. FORBID: AsyncStorage (use MMKV). FORBID: skipping persistence on transitions for performance — debounce 250ms instead. FORBID: silent retry-fail without telemetry — emit onboarding_sync_failed event.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Implement saga for paywall purchase: orchestrate (a) IAP receipt validation via RevenueCat / Apple StoreKit, (b) server entitlement grant via Supabase Edge Function, (c) plan creation, (d) telemetry emit, (e) push-primer trigger. If any step fails, compensate: failed entitlement → log, alert, retry; failed plan create → mark plan generation deferred, allow user to enter app, regenerate later. Output as Temporal workflow OR explicit Promise.allSettled with compensation logic if Temporal unavailable.' Prompt fragment 3: 'Test plan: simulate (a) airplane-mode mid-step, confirm queue + drain, (b) force-quit mid-step, confirm resume on relaunch, (c) reinstall after sign-in, confirm cross-device hydration, (d) schema migration v2→v3, confirm no data loss.'
fields.stitch_application: N/A — architectural, no visual artifact except for the resume-from-correct-step UX. Prompt fragment: 'Mockup the cold-launch experience for a returning user mid-onboarding: splash screen for ~600ms while state hydrates, then directly to step 7 of 12 (their last completed step + 1) with a small toast/banner reading "Welcome back — picking up where you left off" appearing for 2.5s then dismissing. The progress bar reflects step 7. No re-asking of prior questions. Cream background #F4EFE6, ember-orange progress fill #E36B2C.'
fields.ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults to AsyncStorage (slower, async) instead of MMKV. AI doesn't persist on transitions — only on explicit save buttons. AI builds optimistic UI without retry queue. AI ignores app-lifecycle (no scenePhase / Lifecycle hooks). AI doesn't version schema. AI silently swallows network errors. Counter-prompt: 'FORBID @react-native-async-storage/async-storage — use react-native-mmkv. Persist on EVERY FSM transition (debounce 250ms). Implement retry queue in MMKV with exponential backoff (250ms, 1s, 4s) max 3 attempts; persist failed events for next launch drain. Wire iOS scenePhase / Android Lifecycle.ON_PAUSE force-flush. Schema versioning: include version field, write migration function. Network state via NetInfo, pause/resume sync. Emit telemetry on every sync failure (onboarding_sync_failed { reason, attempt, queue_depth }).' AI also under-implements idempotency — counter: 'every external POST includes idempotency_key UUID generated client-side; server checks key in idempotency table, returns prior response if duplicate.'
fields.anti_patterns: (1) AsyncStorage for hot path — slow, blocks UI on RN bridge. (2) Persistence only on explicit save — loses state on crash. (3) No schema versioning — schema changes corrupt resumed state. (4) No idempotency keys — retries cause duplicate state. (5) Silent network errors — user perceives no problem, server has stale state. (6) Server-only or client-only persistence — single point of failure. (7) Saga without compensation — partial transactions leave inconsistent state. (8) Unencrypted sensitive answers — health/identity data in plain MMKV. (9) Unbounded retry queue — battery drain + memory leak on prolonged offline. (10) Forgetting to persist on paywall purchase mid-flow — purchase succeeds, app crashes before grant, user paid for nothing. (11) Assuming app never killed — many real-world scenarios kill apps (memory pressure, OS update, low battery).
fields.regulatory_risk: Medium. (a) GDPR Art. 5(1)(f) integrity & confidentiality — sensitive personal data persisted client-side must be encrypted (Keychain / EncryptedSharedPreferences / MMKV with encryption key). (b) GDPR Art. 17 right to erasure — server endpoint must purge state, client must clear MMKV namespace on user request. (c) GDPR Art. 33 breach notification — local-storage breach (lost device, malware) is a breach if PII unencrypted. (d) HIPAA (if storing health data) — encryption + access logs required. (e) COPPA 2026 — children's data persistence subject to verifiable parental consent, retention limits. (f) Cross-device resume implies cross-device tracking; declare in privacy notice.
fields.platform_constraints_2026: iOS App Store Guideline 5.1.1(v) data minimization — only persist data tied to declared functionality. iOS Background App Refresh + state restoration recommended. iCloud Keychain for cross-device sensitive data. Android Auto Backup for non-sensitive state (default behavior). Android Q+ scoped storage limits external storage. Android 12+ background work restrictions (WorkManager required for retry queues longer than minutes). MMKV is open-source, license MIT.
fields.accessibility_implications: State persistence indirectly aids accessibility — users with cognitive impairments who get distracted mid-flow can return without re-doing work. Loading-from-resume flash should be accessibility-announced ('Welcome back — at step 7 of 12'). VoiceOver/TalkBack focus restoration on resume: programmatically focus the resumed screen's primary interactive element. Avoid jarring visual flash on hydration — use splash bridge.
fields.pitfalls_caveats: (1) MMKV write-on-every-transition can thrash on rapid multi-select toggles — debounce 250ms. (2) Schema migrations must be exhaustively tested across all version pairs. (3) Cross-device merge logic edge cases: simultaneous edits on phone + tablet → last-write-wins might lose data; consider CRDT or vector clocks for true multi-device. (4) Anonymous (pre-auth) state migration to authenticated user state — handle on auth event with merge. (5) Encryption key management on MMKV — key in Keychain / Android Keystore, not hardcoded. (6) State bloat — onboarding state should be <5KB; avoid storing illustrations / large blobs. (7) Battery: aggressive sync on poor network drains; backoff aggressively. (8) Receipt validation across timezones / clock skew — server-side timestamps. (9) FSM snapshot serialization quirks — Date objects don't survive JSON round-trip; use ISO strings or epoch ms. (10) Reanimated SharedValues should NOT be persisted — restart on hydration.
fields.cross_cultural_validity: N/A architectural — applies universally. Locale-specific concerns: low-bandwidth markets (parts of LATAM, Africa, SE Asia) — server-mirror retry must handle long offline windows gracefully, larger MMKV outbound queues. CN: Tencent's MMKV originates here, robustly handles flaky networks. Right-to-erasure (GDPR) variants in CCPA, CPRA, Brazil LGPD, China PIPL — handle per locale.
fields.prescribed_action: Persist FSM state to MMKV on every transition (debounce 250ms). Mirror to server with idempotency keys + exponential-backoff retry queue. Hydrate from MMKV on app launch; merge with server state if authenticated and server newer (cross-device — item 33). Force-flush on iOS scenePhase=background / Android Lifecycle.ON_PAUSE. Schema versioning + migration. Encrypt sensitive answers via Keychain / Keystore-backed key. Use saga (Temporal or hand-rolled) for paywall purchase orchestration. Forbid AsyncStorage, persistence-only-on-explicit-save, silent network failures.
fields.questionnaire_relevance: Direct — answers must persist on every step transition. Loss of any answer breaks personalization stitching (item 14). Resume must restore exact step + answers.
fields.aha_relevance: Indirect — fault-tolerant persistence ensures the AI plan reveal (item 12) is reached without losing context, even if the user's flow was interrupted. Recovers users who would otherwise abandon and not see the Aha.
fields.emotional_archetype_alignment: N/A architectural — but resume-after-interruption with welcome-back microcopy can be archetype-tinted (Caregiver: 'Welcome back — we kept your place'; Magician: 'The thread continues'; Sage: 'Resuming step 7 of 12'; Hero: 'Right where you left off').
fields.anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against 'lost-progress on backgrounding/kill' (the canonical fault-tolerance failure). Pairs with item 30 (FSM) — FSM snapshot IS the persistence unit. Pairs with item 33 (Cross-Device Resume) — server state mirrors client state; resume hydrates from server. Pairs with item 31 (SDUI) — config_version persisted alongside state.
uncertain: specific aggregate conversion lift from persistence (case-study evidence, no RCT)
